title: The Six Swans
lines: A king had six sons and one daughter.
One day a young woman appeared and married the king, but she was really a witch.
She hated the children and cast a spell on the six sons, turning them into white swans during the day.
Only at night could they take their human form.
The daughter, seeing her brothers' plight, determined to save them.
The witch told her that the only way to break the spell was to remain silent for six years and sew six shirts from stinging nettles.
The girl took up the task immediately and worked in silence for six years, suffering great pain from the nettles.
A young prince found her in the forest and fell in love with her.
He took her to his palace, and she continued her work in secret.
Her stepmother convinced the prince that she was a witch and had her condemned to death.
But just as she was about to be burned, the six years were finished and she threw the six shirts over the swans.
They were transformed back into handsome princes.
The witch was revealed and punished, and the princess married the prince.
